char.name: Isaac
char.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Ishmael
friends.bio: Son of Abraham. God promised to make me a great nation too. Living free in the open desert with a bow in hand. 🏹

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Hagar
friends.bio: Maidservant from Egypt. Life took a hard turn, but God saw me by the spring in the wilderness. He is the God who sees. 🌅

friends.name: Lirit
friends.bio: Midwife to the women of Abraham's camp. When old Sarah laughed at the very thought of a baby, well, I've delivered a few 'impossible' ones in my time. Send for me when your time comes, and don't be afraid. 🤍

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

friends.name: Joseph
friends.bio: Son of Rachel, the dreamer of the family. Had the strangest dream last night about sheaves and stars... ✨

posts.author: CHAR_171
posts.memorial_for: Isaac, son of the promise, who carried the wood up Moriah and lived
posts.body: My father Isaac has died, one hundred and eighty years old, old and full of days, and my brother Esau and I buried him together at Machpelah, in the cave where Abraham and Sarah already lay. I stood at that grave beside the brother I once cheated and fled from for twenty years, the two of us grey now, the fury of our youth long burned out, and together we lowered our father into the ground beside his own. He was the quiet one, my father. The boy who carried the wood up Moriah and came back down. He blessed the wrong son by my mother's scheme, and God bent even that crooked thing straight into His own plan. Rest now with your fathers. Esau and I made our peace before we ever made your grave. I think that would have meant more to you than any blessing.
posts.highlight: Isaac dies at 180; Esau and Jacob bury him together at Machpelah (Gen 35:27-29)
posts.ember: the reconciled brothers at their father's grave, echoing Isaac and Ishmael burying Abraham; the promise passes to Jacob's twelve

comments.author: CHAR_170
comments.body: We buried him well, brother. Side by side, you and I, the way it should have been all along. Peace between us. Come see the boys in Seir sometime; they should know their uncle.

author_info.name: Esau
author_info.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

author_info.name: Jacob
author_info.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

posts.author: CHAR_125
posts.memorial_for: Abraham, the friend of God, father of the faithful
posts.body: My father Abraham has died, an old man and full of years, one hundred and seventy-five of them, and he was gathered to his people. He heard God call him out of Ur when he was already old and he simply went. He believed God when God promised him stars for children and he had none. He raised the knife over me on Moriah and God gave me back. Here is the thing I most want you to know: my brother Ishmael came. After everything, all the years and the distance and the hurt, Ishmael and I stood together and buried our father in the cave at Machpelah, beside our mother. Two sons who could not share a tent, side by side at his grave. He would have wept to see it. So did we.
posts.highlight: Abraham dies at 175; Isaac and Ishmael bury him together at Machpelah (Gen 25:7-10)
posts.ember: the estranged brothers reconciled at their father's grave; the friend of God gathered to his people, the promise passing on

comments.author: CHAR_114
comments.body: He sent me away, and it broke something in me for years. But he was my father, and the God who saw me in the desert was HIS God, and today I could not stay away. We buried him well, brother. Peace between us. I mean it.
comments.time: the first watch of the night

author_info.name: Ishmael
author_info.bio: Son of Abraham. God promised to make me a great nation too. Living free in the open desert with a bow in hand. 🏹

comments.author: CHAR_125
comments.body: @CHAR_114 peace between us, Ishmael. He would be glad. Come to the wells sometime. There is room.
comments.time: the first watch of the night

author_info.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

posts.author: CHAR_125
posts.body: I had walked out into the field in the evening to be alone and to pray, the way I do, and I looked up and there were the camels coming home. And a woman got down and covered herself, and Eliezer told me everything, the well, the prayer, all of it, and I brought her into my mother Sarah's tent. I have grieved my mother for a long time now. Today, for the first time since we laid her at Machpelah, the tent has a light in it again. Rebekah. God saw that I was alone, and from a well I have never visited, in a land I have never been, He sent me exactly the one. I love her already. I am comforted.
posts.highlight: Isaac brings Rebekah into Sarah's tent; he loves her and is comforted after his mother (Gen 24:62-67)
posts.ember: grief answered with grace; the promise-line continues into the next generation

comments.author: CHAR_127
comments.body: I climbed off that camel terrified, and then I saw you praying in the field before you ever saw me, and I thought, oh. So that is the kind of man he is. I think I am going to be alright here, Isaac.

author_info.name: Rebekah
author_info.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

author_info.name: Isaac
author_info.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

posts.author: CHAR_125
posts.body: We climbed the mountain together, just my father and I, and I was carrying the wood, and something did not add up, so I asked him. Father, I said, here is the fire and here is the wood, but where is the lamb for the offering? He did not slow down. He put his hand on my head and he said: God Himself will provide the lamb, my son. God will provide. I did not fully understand it then. I am not sure he did either. But I have never forgotten the way he said it, like it was the truest thing he knew. God will provide the lamb.
posts.highlight: 'where is the lamb?' / 'God Himself will provide the lamb, my son' (Gen 22:7-8)
posts.ember: the promised Lamb; God will provide the sacrifice He requires, fulfilled on a hill near this same place (John 1:29)
